mapping.a_element: probe D7-brane with nontrivial worldvolume gauge field in AdS-Schwarzschild background
mapping.b_element: test D-dimensional brane interacting with a bulk black hole in asymptotically flat spacetime

mapping.a_element: effective horizon on the D7-brane worldvolume
mapping.b_element: induced horizon on the brane when it crosses the bulk black hole horizon

mapping.a_element: conductivity tensor extracted from induced current due to external fields
drag force and kinetic mass of charge carriers in the plasma
mapping.b_element: critical solution at the threshold of brane-black-hole formation

brief_justification: While both papers involve probe D-branes in black hole backgrounds and discuss horizons (effective or induced), Paper A focuses on computing a conductivity tensor via AdS/CFT and linear response to external fields, whereas Paper B studies merger transitions and critical phenomena in brane-black-hole systems. The core mechanisms—linear response theory vs. phase transitions and critical scaling—are distinct, despite superficial similarities in the gravitational dual setup.
